title: Splitter
description: Splitter creates resizable horizontal or vertical panel layouts with draggable and keyboard-accessible gutters, size constraints, and support for nested shells.
intro: Use `ui-splitter` for editor shells, sidebars, inspector layouts, dashboard regions, and other work surfaces where users benefit from allocating more space to one region without leaving the current page.
importCode: import { SplitterComponent, SplitterPanelDirective, type SplitterPanel, type SplitterResizeEvent } from 'ui';

featureSections.title: Basic workspace split
featureSections.description: A horizontal three-panel layout is the most common splitter pattern for content plus side regions such as navigation and inspectors.

featureSections.title: Min and max constraints
featureSections.description: Constraints keep fields, lists, and dense views usable. They matter more than the raw resize affordance because they protect the layout from collapsing into unusable states.

featureSections.title: Vertical regions
featureSections.description: Vertical splitters work well for summary-plus-feed, toolbar-plus-content, and top-bottom dashboard layouts.

featureSections.title: Fixed utility panel
featureSections.description: Sometimes one side should stay locked while the neighboring region absorbs resizing. This is useful for narrow rails and persistent utility panels.

featureSections.title: Nested workspace shell
featureSections.description: Nested splitters are appropriate when a real product surface has more than one resizing axis, such as sidebar plus editor plus output panel.

featureSections.title: Keyboard resizing
featureSections.description: The resize handle is keyboard reachable. This matters for accessibility, but it is also useful when users want smaller, more precise adjustments than a pointer drag.

accessibility.title: Group and separator semantics
accessibility.body: `ui-splitter` renders the whole layout as a grouped region and exposes each active gutter as a keyboard-focusable `separator`. The gutter reflects `aria-orientation`, `aria-valuenow`, `aria-valuemin`, and `aria-valuemax`.

accessibility.title: Keyboard behavior
accessibility.body:
The gutter supports keyboard resizing.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Tab / Shift+Tab | Moves focus to or from a resizable gutter. |
| ArrowLeft / ArrowRight | Resize horizontal panels in small steps. |
| ArrowUp / ArrowDown | Resize vertical panels in small steps. |
| Home | Moves the gutter toward the minimum size of the leading panel. |
| End | Moves the gutter toward the minimum size of the trailing panel. |

accessibility.title: Labels and constraints
accessibility.body: Use a meaningful `ariaLabel` when the default splitter label is not specific enough for the surrounding screen. Panel size constraints are not just visual rules; they also prevent keyboard resizing from creating unusable regions.

apiSections.title: SplitterComponent - ui-splitter
apiSections.lead: Standalone resizable layout component for horizontal or vertical panel arrangements. It uses declared panels plus projected templates identified by `uiSplitterPanel`.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Panel configuration array that controls order, initial size, and constraints.
Width of the resize gutter strip in pixels (visual grip stays narrow in the centre).
Accessible label for the splitter group.

blocks.title: Outputs and models
rows: Emits whenever keyboard or pointer resizing updates a panel size.
{ id, size?, minSize?, maxSize?, collapsed?, resizable? }
Panel definition used by the component.
Resize event payload emitted for affected panels.
Associates a projected `ng-template` with a panel id.

paragraphs: Panel `size` values are treated as proportional layout percentages, while `minSize` and `maxSize` are pixel constraints converted against the current container size.
Use nested splitters sparingly. They are powerful for real workspace shells, but too many resize axes can make a layout noisy and hard to stabilize.
blocks.title: Notes
